input.background: Urinary cotinine excretion was measured in nonsmokers who lived with smokers. The following table shows the summary of findings given in the paper (Matsukura et al., 1984).
input.question: What can you say about the shape of the distribution of urinary cotinine in these nonsmokers?

output.answer:
The SDs can be calculated by multiplying the SEs by \(\sqrt{n}\) to give:

\begin{tabular}{clll}
\hline Cigarettes & $n$ & Mean & SD \\
\hline $1-9$ & 25 & 0.31 & 0.40 \\
$10-19$ & 57 & 0.42 & 0.75 \\
$20-29$ & 99 & 0.87 & 1.89 \\
$30-39$ & 38 & 1.03 & 1.54 \\
$>40$ & 28 & 1.56 & 3.02 \\
Unspecified & 25 & 0.56 & 0.80 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

As in all cases the SD is greater than the mean (and negative values are impossible), the urinary cotinine excretion values are highly skewed to the right.
